enonce.texte: Match the family member with the correct definition.

Classify the following family members into Nuclear Family or Extended Family.

💡 Remember:

  • Nuclear Family: father, mother, brother, sister, children
  • Extended Family: grandfather, grandmother, uncle, aunt, cousin, nephew, niece

Look at the family tree and answer the questions.

Family Tree of the Kameni Family

Grandfather
Jean
Grandmother
Marie
Father
Paul
Mother
Sophie
Uncle
Marc
Me
Luc
Sister
Emma
Cousin
Chantal

questions.reponse: Luc's grandparents are Jean and Marie.

questions.texte: How many generations are shown in this family tree? Identify them.
questions.reponse:
There are three generations shown:
1st generation: Grandparents (Jean and Marie)
2nd generation: Parents (Paul and Sophie) and Uncle (Marc)
3rd generation: Children (Luc, Emma, and Chantal)

enonce.texte: Read the descriptions and identify the family member.

questions.texte: She is your mother's mother. Who is she?
He is your father's brother. Who is he?
She is your sister's daughter. Who is she?
He is your uncle's son. Who is he?
She is your aunt's daughter. Who is she?

enonce.texte:
Write 5-6 sentences describing your extended family.

📝 Your description should include:

  • At least 3 members of your extended family (grandparents, uncles, aunts, cousins, etc.)
  • Their relationship to you
  • Their names (if you know them)
  • What they do (their jobs, where they live, etc.)

questions.texte: Write your paragraph about your extended family.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

"I have a big extended family. My grandfather's name is Mr. Ngono. He is seventy years old and he lives in Yaoundé. My grandmother's name is Mrs. Ngono. She is sixty-eight years old. I have two uncles. My uncle Joseph is a doctor and my uncle Marc is a teacher. I also have two aunts. My aunt Marie is a nurse and my aunt Anne is a shopkeeper. I have many cousins. They are all students. I love my extended family because they are very kind."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Correct use of extended family vocabulary
  • Clear description of relationships
  • Correct use of 'to be' and 'to have'
  • Correct use of possessive adjectives (my, his, her, their)
  • Proper grammar and spelling

enonce.texte:
Create a family tree showing your extended family (three generations).

📋 Instructions:

  • Draw a family tree with three generations
  • Include: grandparents, parents, uncles, aunts, cousins, siblings
  • Label each person with their name and relationship
  • Write a short description of your family tree
images: A three-generation family tree showing grandparents, their children (parents and aunts/uncles), and grandchildren (cousins and siblings)

questions.texte: Draw your extended family tree and write a description.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

"My extended family tree has three generations. The first generation is my grandparents. My grandfather is Mr. Bello and my grandmother is Mrs. Bello. The second generation is my parents and my aunts and uncles. My father is Ahmed and my mother is Fatima. My uncle is Ibrahim and my aunt is Aisha. The third generation is my cousins and my siblings. My sister is Mariam and my brother is Youssouf. My cousins are Hawa and Moussa. We are one big family."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Clear family tree with correct labels
  • Three generations represented
  • Correct use of extended family vocabulary
  • Clear description of relationships
  • Proper grammar and spelling

enonce.texte:
Complete the comparison chart and write a paragraph about the differences between nuclear and extended families.

📋 Instructions:

  • Complete the chart with nuclear and extended family members
  • Write a paragraph explaining the differences

questions.texte: Complete the chart and write your comparison paragraph.
questions.reponse:
Example Chart:

Nuclear FamilyExtended Family
FatherGrandfather
MotherGrandmother
BrotherUncle
SisterAunt
ChildrenCousin
-Nephew
-Niece

Example Paragraph:

"A nuclear family is a small family that includes parents and their children. The members are father, mother, brother, and sister. An extended family is a larger family. It includes the nuclear family and other relatives like grandparents, uncles, aunts, and cousins. In Cameroon, many families are extended families. This means that many relatives live together or near each other. I like my extended family because I have many people to play with and learn from."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Correct classification of family members
  • Clear explanation of differences
  • Correct use of vocabulary
  • Proper grammar and spelling
